title: 8 Submitting Bill Payments to Central 1 via a Direct Connection to the eCommSwitch
body_text:
8	Submitting Bill Payments to Central 1 via a Direct Connection to the eCommSwitch
8.1	Overview of Submitting Bill Payments to Central 1 via a Direct Connection to the eCommSwitch
Financial institutions can send bill payments to Central 1 via file transmission or direct connection to Central 1âs eCommSwitch. Bill payments submitted via direct connection can be sent in real-time or at a designated time. If the financial institution uses a gateway, all bill payment information is sent to Central 1 when the gateway is opened at a specific time. Alternatively, the financial institution can allow transactions to flow to Central 1 as they are made (real-time).
Bill payments are forwarded to the billerâs financial institution on the business day after they are made if they are submitted to Central 1 by 11:59:59 pm PT / 2:59:59 am ET. Payments submitted after the deadline will be forwarded to the billerâs financial institution one business day later than those submitted before the deadline.
Important
:	Bill payments are subject to cut-off times that vary by submission method or biller. See Section 1.4, Bill Payment Cut-Off Times and Billers with Non-Standard Pricing, for more information.
The direct connection option is available to financial institutions in all regions.
8.2	Validating Electronic Bill Payments Sent via Direct Connection
Central 1 holds the electronic bill payments database for most of the financial institutions that send bill payment transactions to Central 1 via direct connection to the eCommSwitch. Biller account numbers are pre-validated when the customer sets up a biller account. Bill payments are validated when they are received at Central 1.
Central 1 rejects transactions that:
Fail the billerâs account number validation routine
Are duplicate payments (i.e., two or more transactions made to the same biller, the same biller account number, for the same amount from the same customer account number within a 24-hour period)
If Central 1 rejects a bill payment, the transaction is reported on the BPRT, Bill Payments Rejected Transaction report. See Chapter 9, Electronic Bill Payment Reports, and Chapter 11, Reconciliation of Electronic Bill Payments.
Note:	Transactions can be rejected by the biller even if they pass Central 1âs validation process.
END
